title: cyanea pilosa subsp. pilosa
intro: Cyanea pilosa subsp. pilosa, also known as the Kaua'i cyanea or Kaua'i wiliwili, is a rare and endangered plant species endemic to the island of Kaua'i in Hawaii. This plant is traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Cyanea pilosa subsp. pilosa include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to treat wounds and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are typically used to make teas, infusions, and salves that can be applied topically or taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
